name: Yuze Zheng
title: PhD Candidate in Chemistry
shortIntro: I work on bioelectronics, optoelectronic neuromodulation, and semiconductor-biology interfaces. Outside the lab, I use photography to document wildlife, city life, and quiet moments around Chicago.
biography: Yuze Zheng is a PhD candidate in Chemistry at the University of Chicago. His research focuses on functional interfaces between optoelectronic materials and biological systems.

His work explores bioelectronics, optoelectronic neuromodulation, and semiconductor-biology interfaces, with an emphasis on how materials, interfaces, and light-driven processes can be used to communicate with living systems.
Outside the lab, he photographs wildlife, urban landscapes, and everyday scenes around Chicago.

selectedResearchDirections.title: Optoelectronic biointerfaces
selectedResearchDirections.description: Light-addressable materials and devices for interacting with biological systems.

selectedResearchDirections.title: Semiconductor-biology interfaces
selectedResearchDirections.description: Functional interfaces that bridge inorganic materials and living systems.

selectedResearchDirections.title: Plasmonic materials
selectedResearchDirections.description: Nanostructured optical materials for controlling light-driven interfacial processes.
